label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 16

extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked on the final 4.8km climb to Beires, dropped KUSS by 1:05 and reduced GC gap to 29 seconds.
tactical_patterns: GROVES maximized intermediate sprint points before abandoning breakaway pre-climb, targeting green jersey through non-contested intermediates rather than finishes.
ROGLIC checked for KUSS position post-finish, revealing intra-team tension; finished 4 seconds ahead suggesting cooperation ceiling reached at 29-second margin.
VINGEGAARD attacked with 4.8km remaining on 14-16% gradients, reversing Tour fatigue narrative and establishing psychological dominance before Angliru.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Tour de France fatigue narrative appears false; attacked decisively on steep finale taking 36 seconds from Kuss
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: lost 1:05 on short steep climb despite team support, GC lead shrinking to 29 seconds reveals vulnerability on extreme gradients
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: finished between Vingegaard and Kuss with deliberate shoulder-check, suggesting team tactics now subordinate to individual GC ambitions
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: tactically maximized sprint points in breakaway before abandoning pre-climb, exploiting uncontested intermediate sprints for green jersey accumulation
rider_signals.FISHER-BLACK Finn: positive: UAE domestique finished second on steep finish, unexpected climbing strength for support role
